title: Musicians
description: Formulate design concepts and presentation approaches for visual productions and media, such as print, broadcasting, video, and film. Direct workers engaged in artwork or layout design. Produce or direct stage, television, radio, video, or film productions for entertainment, information, or instruction. Responsible for creative decisions, such as interpretation of script, choice of actors or guests, set design, sound, special effects, and choreography. Direct and coordinate activities of personnel engaged in preparation of radio or television station program schedules and programs, such as sports or news. (Based on 12 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Interview production and engineering personnel and read journals to become familiar with product technologies and production methods.
Read, evaluate, and edit manuscripts or other materials submitted for publication, and confer with authors regarding changes.
Meet frequently with artists, typesetters, layout personnel, and production managers to discuss projects and resolve problems.
Supervise and coordinate work of reporters, editors, and personnel engaged in broadcast news, sports, or programming.
Direct and coordinate activities of personnel engaged in the preparation of program schedules and broadcasts.
Test equipment to ensure proper operation and monitor broadcasts to ensure compliance with policies and regulations.
Conduct interviews and auditions for performers, reviewing information to decide whom to audition for specific roles.
Plan and schedule programming and event coverage based on community needs, ratings data, and viewer demographics.
Confer with clients, directors, and production staff to discuss project objectives, budgets, and presentation approaches.
Prepare, rewrite, and edit copy to improve readability, or supervise others who do this work.
Select and maintain programs and legal clearances for use in productions while reviewing for accuracy and content quality.
Develop and present ideas for programs and features that a station could produce, and monitor programming for adherence to guidelines.

Coordinate logistics for productions or events.

Operate communications, transmissions, or broadcasting equipment.

Review details of technical drawings or specifications.
Confer with clients to determine needs.
Obtain copyrights or other legal permissions.
Design layout of art or product exhibits, displays, or promotional materials.
Maintain records, documents, or other files.
Stay informed about current developments in field of specialization.
Develop operating strategies, plans, or procedures.
Present work to clients for approval.
Interview others for news or entertainment purposes.
Monitor broadcasting operations to ensure proper functioning.
Develop artistic or design concepts for decoration, exhibition, or commercial purposes.
Set up still or video cameras or related equipment.
Operate control consoles for sound, lighting or video.
Develop proposals for current or prospective customers.
Confer with coworkers to coordinate work activities.
Collaborate with others to develop or refine designs.
Teach classes in area of specialization.
Teach humanities courses at the college level.
Support the professional development of others.
Maintain inventories of materials, equipment, or products.
Collaborate with others in marketing activities.

Respond to questions from the public.
Write text, such as headlines, stories, articles, editorials, or newsletters.
